responses.question: Could you briefly introduce yourself — your program, year of study, and how long you have been at HKUST(GZ)?
How would you describe the general social environment at HKUST(GZ)?
How would you describe your interactions with international students at HKUST(GZ)?
Do you think it is easy or difficult for Chinese and international students to become friends here? Why?
responses.answer: Relatively easy; there are many international students

responses.question: In your experience, do Chinese and international students mix naturally, or do they mostly stay in separate circles? Why?
responses.answer: Stay in separate circles; mainly because of language

responses.question: What factors make interaction between Chinese and international students easier?
To what extent do language or cultural differences affect communication between Chinese and international students?
responses.answer: To some extent; students tend to make friends with those who share similar language and culture, as communication is smoother

responses.question: In your opinion, does social integration affect international students’ academic experiences? How?
responses.answer: Yes; UFUG courses are more difficult

responses.question: Have you noticed any difference in your academic performance between UCUG and UFUG courses? Why?
From what you have observed, do international students seem to perform differently in UCUG and UFUG courses? Why?
Do you think international students sometimes face disadvantages in group work, class participation, or access to academic support because of weaker social connections? Why or why not?
Have you noticed any differences in how Chinese and international students participate in group projects, study groups, or classroom interaction?
responses.answer: No; I have a fairly active social life

responses.question: Do you think students with stronger social networks generally perform better academically at HKUST(GZ)? Why?
What do you think are the biggest barriers preventing stronger integration between Chinese and international students?
Do you think the university does enough to encourage interaction and inclusion between different student groups? Why or why not?
responses.answer: Course selection and dormitory arrangement make integration harder

responses.question: Are there any university practices, campus habits, or classroom dynamics that unintentionally make integration harder?
What do you think HKUST(GZ) could do better to improve integration between Chinese and international students?

summary_bullets: Social environment perceived as average; made relatively few friends initially.
Thinks Chinese and international students tend to stay in separate circles mainly because of language.
Reports interacting with international students quite a lot.
Believes UFUG courses are more difficult.
Does not think international students are academically disadvantaged; sees classmates as friendly.
Identifies language and culture as central barriers.
Feels course selection and dormitory arrangements make integration harder.
